multiprocessing.sharedctypes/BaseManager.name: address
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/BaseManager.description: The address used by the manager.

info.excerpt: The address used by the manager.

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Listener/ad.name: address
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Listener/ad.description: The address which is being used by the Listener object.

info.excerpt: The address which is being used by the Listener object.

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Process/aut.name: authkey
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Process/aut.description: The process’s authentication key (a byte string). When multiprocessing is initialized the main process is assigned a random string using os.urandom(). When a Process object is created, it will inherit the authentication key of its parent process, although this may be changed by setting authkey to another byte string. See Authentication keys.

info.excerpt: The process’s authentication key (a byte string). When multiprocessing is initialized the main process is assigned a random string using os.urandom(). When a Process object is created, it will inherit...

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/AsyncResult.name: AsyncResult
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/AsyncResult.description: The class of the result returned by Pool.apply_async() and Pool.map_async(). get([timeout])¶ Return the result when it arrives. If timeout is not None and the result does not arrive within timeout seconds then multiprocessing.TimeoutError is raised. If the remote call raised an exception then that exception will be reraised by get(). wait([timeout])¶ Wait until the result is available or until timeout seconds pass. ready()¶ Return whether the call has completed. successful()¶ Return whether t...

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Authenticat.name: AuthenticationError
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Authenticat.description: Raised when there is an authentication error.

info.excerpt: Raised when there is an authentication error.

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Array.name: Array
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Array.description: Return a ctypes array allocated from shared memory. By default the return value is actually a synchronized wrapper for the array. typecode_or_type determines the type of the elements of the returned array: it is either a ctypes type or a one character typecode of the kind used by the array module with the exception of 'w', which is not supported. In addition, the 'c' typecode is an alias for ctypes.c_char. If size_or_initializer is an integer, then it determines the length of the array, and t...

info.excerpt: Return a ctypes array allocated from shared memory. By default the return value is actually a synchronized wrapper for the array. typecode_or_type determines the type of the elements of the returned a...

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/active_chil.name: active_children
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/active_chil.description: Return list of all live children of the current process. Calling this has the side effect of “joining” any processes which have already finished.

info.excerpt: Return list of all live children of the current process. Calling this has the side effect of “joining” any processes which have already finished.

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/answer_chal.name: answer_challenge
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/answer_chal.description: Receive a message, calculate the digest of the message using authkey as the key, and then send the digest back. If a welcome message is not received, then AuthenticationError is raised.

info.excerpt: Receive a message, calculate the digest of the message using authkey as the key, and then send the digest back. If a welcome message is not received, then AuthenticationError is raised.

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/SyncManager.name: Array
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/SyncManager.description: Create an array and return a proxy for it.

info.excerpt: Create an array and return a proxy for it.

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Listener/ac.name: accept
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Listener/ac.description: Accept a connection on the bound socket or named pipe of the listener object and return a Connection object. If authentication is attempted and fails, then AuthenticationError is raised.

info.excerpt: Accept a connection on the bound socket or named pipe of the listener object and return a Connection object. If authentication is attempted and fails, then AuthenticationError is raised.

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Lock/acquir.name: acquire
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Lock/acquir.description: Acquire a lock, blocking or non-blocking. With the block argument set to True (the default), the method call will block until the lock is in an unlocked state, then set it to locked and return True. Note that the name of this first argument differs from that in threading.Lock.acquire(). With the block argument set to False, the method call does not block. If the lock is currently in a locked state, return False; otherwise set the lock to a locked state and return True. When invoked with a pos...

info.excerpt: Acquire a lock, blocking or non-blocking. With the block argument set to True (the default), the method call will block until the lock is in an unlocked state, then set it to locked and return True. N...

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/RLock/acqui.name: acquire
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/RLock/acqui.description: Acquire a lock, blocking or non-blocking. When invoked with the block argument set to True, block until the lock is in an unlocked state (not owned by any process or thread) unless the lock is already owned by the current process or thread. The current process or thread then takes ownership of the lock (if it does not already have ownership) and the recursion level inside the lock increments by one, resulting in a return value of True. Note that there are several differences in this first arg...

info.excerpt: Acquire a lock, blocking or non-blocking. When invoked with the block argument set to True, block until the lock is in an unlocked state (not owned by any process or thread) unless the lock is already...

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Pool/apply.name: apply
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Pool/apply.description: Call func with arguments args and keyword arguments kwds. It blocks until the result is ready. Given this blocks, apply_async() is better suited for performing work in parallel. Additionally, func is only executed in one of the workers of the pool.

multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Pool/apply_.name: apply_async
multiprocessing.sharedctypes/Pool/apply_.description: A variant of the apply() method which returns a AsyncResult object. If callback is specified then it should be a callable which accepts a single argument. When the result becomes ready callback is applied to it, that is unless the call failed, in which case the error_callback is applied instead. If error_callback is specified then it should be a callable which accepts a single argument. If the target function fails, then the error_callback is called with the exception instance.
